mnemonics: A Very Important Transaction Is Confirmed On Ledger - AVITICOL: Authorization, Validation, Inclusion in Block, Transmission, Inclusion in Block, Confirmation, On-chain, Ledger Update
keyPoints: A transaction is created and cryptographically signed with a private key to authorize it.
The transaction is broadcast to the peer-to-peer network where nodes validate its authenticity and correctness.
Miners/Validators group valid transactions into a new block and perform Proof-of-Work (or other consensus) to append it to the chain, providing confirmation.
commonMistakes: Believing a transaction is final as soon as it is broadcast; it is only final after several confirmations (blocks deep).
Confusing transaction validation (checking signatures, inputs) with block confirmation (solving the cryptographic puzzle).
